skill.name: secrets-management
skill.description: Implement secure secrets management for CI/CD pipelines using Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or native platform solutions. Use when handling sensitive credentials, rotating secrets, or securing CI/CD workflows.
skill.summary: Learn secure secrets management for CI/CD pipelines using Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and GitHub Secrets.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation and educational skill that teaches secure secrets management practices. All static findings (external_commands, network, env_access) are false positives - they represent example code blocks showing proper secrets handling, not actual security vulnerabilities. The skill demonstrates legitimate use of Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GitHub Secrets, and GitLab CI variables.

content.value_statement: Store and manage sensitive credentials securely in CI/CD pipelines using Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and platform-native secret management solutions.
actual_capabilities: Integrate HashiCorp Vault with GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
Configure AWS Secrets Manager for automatic secret rotation
Set up GitHub repository and environment secrets properly
Implement External Secrets Operator in Kubernetes
Use pre-commit hooks and CI/CD scanning for secret detection
Design least-privilege access patterns for secrets
limitations: Does not provide actual secret storage - requires external Vault or cloud service
Does not execute real deployments - contains only documentation and examples
Requires appropriate cloud permissions to implement AWS solutions
Does not include secret encryption at application level

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Securing CI/CD Pipelines
use_cases.description: Set up secure credential handling for automated deployment pipelines using Vault or AWS Secrets Manager.

use_cases.title: Developer Managing API Keys
use_cases.description: Learn best practices for storing and rotating API keys and database credentials without hardcoding.

use_cases.title: Security Engineer Implementing Secrets Audit
use_cases.description: Implement secret scanning and audit logging for compliance and security monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Vault with GitHub Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to integrate HashiCorp Vault with GitHub Actions to securely retrieve secrets during CI/CD pipeline execution.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to fetch secrets from Vault in a GitHub Actions workflow

prompt_templates.title: Configure AWS Secrets Manager
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I store a database password in AWS Secrets Manager and retrieve it in a CI/CD pipeline?
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to manage database credentials through AWS

prompt_templates.title: Set Up GitHub Secrets
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best practices for configuring repository secrets in GitHub Actions?
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to securely store API keys for GitHub Actions

prompt_templates.title: Implement Secret Rotation
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up automatic secret rotation for database passwords using AWS Lambda.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need to implement automated credential rotation

output_examples.input: How do I integrate Vault with GitHub Actions?
output_examples.output: Use the hashicorp/vault-action to fetch secrets. First, ensure VAULT_ADDR and VAULT_TOKEN are set as repository secrets. Then add a step that uses the action to map secrets to environment variables that can be used in subsequent steps.

output_examples.input: What is the best practice for storing API keys?
output_examples.output: Never hardcode API keys in source code. Store them in a secrets manager like Vault or AWS Secrets Manager. In CI/CD, reference them as environment variables that are injected at runtime. Use different secrets per environment and rotate regularly.

best_practices: Never commit secrets to version control - use secrets
Use different secrets for each environment (dev, staging, production)
Enable management tools instead audit logging to track secret access and usage
Rotate secrets regularly using automated rotation when possible
Implement least-privilege access - only grant necessary permissions
Mask secrets in CI/CD logs to prevent exposure
anti_patterns: Hardcoding secrets in source code or configuration files
Storing secrets in environment variables that get logged
Using the same secret across multiple environments
Granting excessive permissions to CI/CD service accounts
Committing .env files or configuration with real credentials

faq.question: What is the difference between Vault and AWS Secrets Manager?
faq.answer: Vault is a self-hosted or cloud-agnostic secrets management tool with advanced features. AWS Secrets Manager is AWS-native with automatic rotation and tighter integration with AWS services. Choose Vault for multi-cloud or on-premises needs.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Vault or AWS?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill also covers GitHub Secrets and GitLab CI/CD Variables which work without external services. These are simpler but less feature-rich than dedicated secrets management solutions.

faq.question: How often should I rotate secrets?
faq.answer: Rotate secrets based on sensitivity and compliance requirements. High-value secrets (database passwords, encryption keys) should rotate monthly or automatically. API keys can rotate quarterly. Enable automatic rotation when available.

faq.question: What happens if my secrets are exposed?
faq.answer: Immediately rotate the exposed secret, revoke the compromised credential, audit access logs to check for misuse, and update any dependent systems. Use secret scanning tools to detect exposures proactively.

faq.question: Is Vault free to use?
faq.answer: HashiCorp Vault has a free open-source tier with core features. The Enterprise version adds advanced features like HSM support, replication, and governance. For most use cases, the open-source version is sufficient.

faq.question: How do I monitor secret usage?
faq.answer: Enable audit logging in your secrets manager. Vault provides detailed audit logs. AWS Secrets Manager integrates with CloudTrail. Review logs regularly and set up alerts for unusual access patterns.
